notes: A late notice only reaches back 20 days from when it is served.

trigger: completion of the work of improvement
notes: 90 days after completion. If the owner records a Notice of Completion/Cessation, a direct contractor has only 60 days.

served_on: owner, prime contractor, and construction lender (if any)
notes: A late notice only reaches back 20 days from when it is served.

trigger: completion of the work of improvement
notes: 90 days after completion. If the owner records a Notice of Completion/Cessation, a subcontractor has only 30 days.

trigger: month of last labor or materials furnished
notes: Original contractors do not send monthly pre-lien notices, but must record the affidavit by this deadline.

trigger: last day a claimant could file the lien affidavit
notes: Reduced from 2 years to 1 year for projects on/after 2022-01-01. May be extended to 2 years only by written agreement with the current owner.

trigger: each month unpaid labor or materials furnished
notes: Send a separate notice for each unpaid month. The notice lets the owner trap funds owed to the general contractor.

trigger: month of last labor or materials furnished
notes: Commercial: 15th day of the 4th calendar month. Residential: 15th day of the 3rd calendar month.

trigger: last day a claimant could file the lien affidavit
notes: Reduced from 2 years to 1 year for projects on/after 2022-01-01.

trigger: final furnishing of labor or materials
notes: Warranty/punch-list/remedial work does NOT extend the 90-day clock.
A Notice of Contest of Lien can shorten this to 60 days.
Must be served within 45 days of first furnishing AND before the owner makes final payment. Missing the NTO forfeits lien rights.

trigger: final furnishing of labor or materials
